text: In the operating room, after induction of general anesthesia, his head was immobilized in a skull clamp, turned approximately 30 degrees away from the side of the approach, and angled for near-orthogonal approach to the tumor, with gravity keeping the tumor along the axis of the surgical approach.

Bipolar cautery is used to create a small cortisectomy through which the superficial edge of the tumor will be exposed.
The general plan is to remove a relatively large tumor through a small corridor and therefore minimize damage to the brain.
